mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate breaches the 4.0% threshold, labor market slack triggers recession fears that override growth expectations, causing sustained negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.0%, or the threshold effect loses statistical significance (p>0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer prices indicate robust demand pulling through inflation, which forces central banks to maintain accommodative policy longer, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in greater than 50% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p>0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals immediate capacity utilization gains that boost short-term corporate profit expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during expansionary phases, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation (Rank IC < 0.01) with INDPRO momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms economic resilience and supports consumer spending forecasts, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth consistently coincides with negative 20-day equity returns, or the signal out-of-sample hit rate falls below 45% across rolling validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4% triggers immediate expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following CPI prints above 0.4% in greater than 55% of cases, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for prior rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining housing starts and rising retail sales indicates a shift from durable goods to services consumption, signaling weakening aggregate demand that depresses equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern consistently predicts positive returns, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit (AIC/BIC) over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms strong macroeconomic momentum that sustains corporate earnings trajectories, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter during stable policy environments.
failure_condition: GDP prints above 2.5% are followed by negative quarterly equity returns in greater than 60% of observations, or the signal loses predictive power during volatile rate cycles.
